transcript_facts:
*   The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.
*   The patient denies blood in stool.
*   The clinician suspects cervical radiculopathy.
*   The clinician plans a cervical MRI.
*   The patient reports improved walking ability after surgery.
*   The patient reports bladder control has improved but is now wearing diapers.
*   The patient reports feeling better able to have normal conversations.
*   The patient reports no headaches or pain related to the shunt.
*   The patient reports no belly pain.
*   The patient reports a tiny bit of blood fluid collection in the right parietal region.
*   The patient is unlikely to be able to pass a driver safety evaluation.
*   The patient is considering enrolling in a driver safety program.
*   The patient has a gait score of six to eight out of 12 on the Tinetti assessment tool.
*   The patient has a balance score of 12 out of 16 on the Tinetti assessment tool.
*   The clinician notes a frontal horn span of 4.6 centimeters with a third ventricular contour of 10 millimeters.
*   The clinician notes a tiny subdermal collection in the right frontal region.
*   The clinician notes substantial small vessel ischemic change.
source_block:
- The patient reports fatigue for the past two months.
- The patient denies blood in stool.
- The clinician suspects cervical radiculopathy.
- The clinician plans a cervical MRI.
- The patient reports improved walking ability after surgery.
- The patient reports bladder control has improved but is now wearing diapers.
- The patient reports feeling better able to have normal conversations.
- The patient reports no headaches or pain related to the shunt.
- The patient reports no belly pain.
- The patient reports a tiny bit of blood fluid collection in the right parietal region.
- The patient is unlikely to be able to pass a driver safety evaluation.
- The patient is considering enrolling in a driver safety program.
- The patient has a gait score of six to eight out of 12 on the Tinetti assessment tool.
- The patient has a balance score of 12 out of 16 on the Tinetti assessment tool.
- The clinician notes a frontal horn span of 4.6 centimeters with a third ventricular contour of 10 millimeters.
- The clinician notes a tiny subdermal collection in the right frontal region.
- The clinician notes substantial small vessel ischemic change.
